meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Overcome Tutorial Hell via Project Management
content.seo_description: Stop consuming tutorials and start shipping. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to master project management and launch your MVP faster. Expert guide for Founders.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start architecting. By shifting from passive consumption to proactive project management, you force your brain to solve real-world constraints. Use Antigravity to define your schema and Claude Code to execute the build, ensuring every line of code serves your product’s revenue model.
content.intro:
You have spent months watching video tutorials, accumulating a graveyard of half-baked 'to-do' lists and unfinished clones. It feels like progress, but your bank account knows the truth: you are not building a business; you are consuming content. The dopamine hit of finishing a video is a deceptive substitute for the grit of shipping a product.

The bridge between 'aspiring dev' and 'profitable founder' is not another course—it is the discipline of project management. By treating your code as an asset and your time as capital, you transform abstract tutorials into a concrete, monetizable engine. We are going to strip away the fluff and use high-leverage tools to force your MVP into existence.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core problem you are solving. Focus on the 'One Thing' that earns revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a lean MVP schema for a SaaS that solves [Insert Niche Problem]. Focus only on the authentication, core data model, and payment integration. Exclude all non-essential features.

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: A solid data model prevents technical debt. We use Antigravity to visualize the relationships between users, subscriptions, and core data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a relational database schema for my MVP. Include tables for users, subscription_status, and core_data_objects. Ensure RLS policies are included for security.

steps.title: Scaffold the Core API
steps.description: Stop building front-ends first. Build the API layer that manages your data. This ensures your business logic is sound.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the boilerplate for a secure REST API using Node.js and TypeScript that interacts with the schema generated in step 2.

steps.title: Integrate Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: If you aren't charging, you are building a hobby. Integrate Stripe immediately to validate your pricing model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription checkout flow module for my API. Include webhooks to handle subscription_updated and invoice_paid events.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Deployment is the final stage of project management. Get your MVP live to start collecting user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready CI/CD configuration for Vercel/Railway that runs tests before deployment.

pro_tips: Treat your codebase like a startup: delete any feature that doesn't directly contribute to your core value proposition.
Use Claude Code to write tests before features; it prevents the 'feature creep' that keeps you in tutorial hell.
Always build your database schema first; your data structure is your business model in code form.
Automate your feedback loop by integrating a simple telemetry logger early in the development process.
Ignore 'perfect' code. Your goal is a 'Minimum Viable Product' that survives long enough to get your first paying customer.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on customer discovery and 20% on refining the code based on actual usage data.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop watching and start shipping. Don't let your code sit on a local machine; put it in front of users and start generating revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
